description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a reddish-brown coloration and a rough, natural stone texture. The block features some irregularities, such as a chipped or indented edge and subtle surface undulations, giving it an authentic quarried appearance. The surface displays visible mineral veins and color variations, suggesting it is made from a type of sandstone or similar sedimentary rock. The block's faces are mostly flat but not perfectly smooth, with some minor imperfections and weathering marks. Its size and shape make it suitable for use as a barrier or boundary element in urban environments, such as parks or sidewalks. There are no visible engravings, markings, or artificial features on its surface.
description: A reddish-brown, rough-textured rectangular stone block with natural surface imperfections and mineral veins. It appears solid and weathered, suitable for use as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: The block is seen from a short end, highlighting a chipped edge and the rough, uneven surface texture.
description_view_1: This long side view shows the full length of the block, with a slight indentation along the top edge and visible mineral streaks.
description_view_2: The opposite short end is visible, showing a more intact edge and the same rough, stone-like surface.
description_view_3: The other long side is shown, with a relatively even surface and prominent reddish-brown coloration.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor surface chipping and subtle color variation suggest some exposure to the elements, but no significant erosion or moss growth is visible.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned with other blocks as a barrier
